g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about deploying smart contracts on a blockchain. This is a fundamental skill for blockchain developers.
g.text: Deploying Smart Contracts on a Blockchain

g.data-narration: Smart contracts are self-executing programs stored on a blockchain that automatically enforce agreement terms when conditions are met.
g.text: Self-executing contracts with terms written directly into code.
They automatically execute when predefined conditions are met.

g.data-narration: Smart contracts eliminate intermediaries, reduce costs, increase transparency, and enable trustless transactions across industries.
g.text: • Provide transparency, security, and trustless execution

g.data-narration: The deployment process has four main steps: writing code, compiling to bytecode, deploying to blockchain, and instantiating the contract.
First, developers write smart contracts in languages like Solidity for Ethereum or Chaincode for Hyperledger Fabric.
The code is compiled into bytecode, which is the low-level executable format that the blockchain's virtual machine can understand.
Deploying sends a transaction containing the compiled bytecode to the blockchain, creating the contract on-chain.
g.text: Result: Contract address is generated upon successful mining

g.data-narration: Instantiation creates the contract instance with initial state. The constructor runs once, setting up the starting values.
Three major platforms support smart contracts: Ethereum uses EVM, Hyperledger Fabric uses chaincode, and Corda uses CorDapps.
Essential deployment tools include Truffle for Ethereum development, Web3.js for blockchain interaction, and Fabric SDK for Hyperledger.
Gas is the computational unit that powers smart contract execution. It measures work done and prevents infinite loops on the network.
Key takeaways: smart contracts are self-executing, deployment has four steps, platforms include Ethereum/Hyperledger/Corda, and tools include Truffle and Web3.js.

g.text: Gas limits computation and prevents abuse
Security testing is critical before deployment

g.data-narration: For exams, remember the four deployment steps, major platforms and tools, gas concepts, and practice using Web3.js for contract management.
